select_when: Decisions, outputs, or model behavior must be explained clearly enough that others can inspect how the conclusion was reached.
The cost of error is high enough that the organization needs a recoverable trail from evidence to judgment to action.
Trust depends not only on being right, but on being able to show what happened, why it happened, and who changed what.
A process is getting too complex to trust by intuition alone and needs a reconstructable causal chain linking inputs, assumptions, and outcomes.
avoid_when: Traceability is becoming performative documentation that consumes attention without improving judgment, accountability, or learning.
The team is treating an auditable process as a linear mechanical blueprint even though the real work still needs interpretation and adaptive judgment.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the model centered on reconstructable reasoning trails, trust through inspectability, and accountability visibility rather than broad governance process language.
donor_drops: Did not preserve donor documentation sprawl because the raw source is stronger on visible causal chains, clear workplanning, and anti-performative traceability discipline.
open_questions: The output type compresses a source that spans strategy, AI systems, communication, and organizational learning.

select_when.note: The raw source explicitly names inspectable conclusions, high error-cost contexts, trust through explanation, and reconstructable causal chains.

avoid_when.note: Both anti-activation conditions are directly grounded in the source warnings about performative documentation and over-mechanized process thinking.

input_type.note: The source activates on explainability and responsibility-trail needs; the compact input label is reviewed normalization.

output_type.note: The source naturally yields reconstructable audit trails, though the typed output label remains a compact reviewed abstraction.

reasoning_types.note: Auditability traceability is explicitly about logical reconstruction and system-level accountability, while the taxonomy labels remain normalized.
